behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure can occur at the handoff between git status extraction and report categorization (e.g., rename not detected and emitted as add+delete). REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: this behavior depends on real git rename detection and subprocess output shape; a stubbed diff can mask wrong git invocation flags or rename-threshold behavior. Rejection-gate: Integration was rejected because the behavior text requires true rename semantics from the analyzed repository, which is controlled by real git behavior, not only in-process wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI against that repo via subprocess, parse stdout report, assert `renamed=1` and `added=0`,`deleted=0` for that change set.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Crashes often arise from real git binary diff output/encoding and CLI parsing boundaries. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: fake text fixtures may not reproduce actual binary-diff markers or subprocess stdout/stderr behavior, so mocks can pass while real runs fail. Rejection-gate: Integration was rejected because the behavior explicitly targets binary diffs in real repository analysis, which is a subprocess/output-format boundary.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp git repo, commit baseline, add/modify a binary file, run CLI as a subprocess, assert exit code is success and report includes binary file in the appropriate count/category.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The key failure is often unborn-HEAD git command behavior (non-zero exit, specific stderr) crossing into CLI error handling. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: only a real no-commit repo and real git subprocess reliably reproduce this boundary. Rejection-gate: Integration was rejected because stubbing git responses would bypass the real empty-history subprocess failure mode this behavior is about.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new git repo with no commits, run CLI subprocess, assert success exit and structured empty report on stdout (no exception text).

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This validates end-to-end correctness against true repository history, requiring interaction across history retrieval, classification, aggregation, and output. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: mocked histories can mirror implementation assumptions and miss mismatches caused by real git history traversal semantics. Rejection-gate: Integration was rejected because behavior text anchors correctness to the actual repository history, which requires real git/repo execution.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo with a controlled sequence of commits containing add/delete/rename/modify operations, run CLI, and assert reported counts exactly match the known history totals.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This failure appears at the boundary between git index/worktree state and the tool’s data collection path. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: in-process fakes of staged/unstaged state can miss real git index semantics and command differences. Rejection-gate: Integration was rejected because behavior explicitly compares real staged vs unstaged repository states, a subprocess/git-state boundary.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create equivalent change sets once staged and once unstaged, run CLI for each scenario via subprocess, and assert byte-for-byte identical structured stdout.
